subject: [DCAE] Updates to defaults for configmap
commit_message:
[DCAE] Updates to defaults for configmap

Make external configMaps optional by
default for microservices
Make applicationConfig an optional parameter
in values.yaml for microservices.

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2770
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2782
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflos@sonoris.net>
Change-Id: I80ec8e4a1d55201c5a174f50159bac171953b031

It's hard to tell from the error message what volume or what volume mount is causing the problem here.  The filebeat container is running, so the filebeat container mounts must be OK.  The policy handler container has 2 volume mounts: the log volume (shared with filebeat) at "/app/logs" and the volume holding TLS certificate materials at "/app/etc/tls/certs/".  The associated volumes are emptyDir volumes.  The error is complaining that the destination of the mount is "logs" and isn't an absolute path.

We see the same error in the daily master run from 2021-06-24, which is the last one I can find:
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/daily/onap_daily_pod4_master/2021-06/24_06-08/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-dcae-policy-handler-9d89bdc5-csgks.html

There was a change back in March, changing the mount path for the volumes mounts from
/opt/app/policy_handler/logs to /app/logs
and
/opt/app/policy_handler/etc/tls/certs/ to /app/etc/tls/certs/
along with a change to the policy handler version.
Seems too long ago to be making a difference now.

Is there any possibility of getting access to the following?
  -- The rendered charts that are driving the gating and integration runs.  I'm interested in these because the error message in the logs complains that the mount "destination" is not absolute.  I'm wondering if somehow the value in the charts "/app/logs" is being changed to "logs" somehow.
  -- The contents of the /var/lib/docker/volumes/ subdirectory that the error message identifies as the "source" of the mount.  This might give a clue about what content cannot be mounted.

I looked at the environment where Vijay has reproduced the problem.  I didn't see any good clues.  The charts are properly rendered.  The /var/lib/docker/.... directory (the "source" of the mount) is empty, which is to be expected for an emptyDir volume.   I tried editing the k8s Deployment to change the mountPath from "/app/logs" to "/app/logs/", but this did not get rid of the problem.  (I'm still not entirely certain what mount the error message is talking about.  There are two volume mounts on the dcae-policy-handler container, one for the logs and one for the TLS certificate info.  I'm guessing, based on the name "logs" in the error message, that it's the log volume mount.  But I could be wrong.)

I don't think PVs/PVCs are an issue here, since we're only using emptyDir volumes on the policy handler container.

I did not that the environment where Vijay is running and the environment where I ran my tests are running different versions of containerd (1.4.4 in Vijay's environment, 1.3.3 in mine) and runc (1.0.0~rc95 in Vijay's environment, 1.0.2-dev in mine).

Patch Set 11:

On further investigation, I have found that the "logs" volume mentioned in the error message is *not* one of the two volume mounts explicitly mentioned in the Helm chart for the policy handler deployment.  It's a spurious volume defined in the Dockerfile:
VOLUME logs
It's present in Vijay's environment and it's present in mine.  But apparently my environment can handle the relative path and Vijay's (and gating) cannot.  As far as I can tell, this volume is not needed for anything, and we should drop it from the Dockerfile.

[How to see this:  First, do a kubectl describe pod and get the Container ID for the container.  (You don't need the docker:// part, just the first few characters of the hex string).  Then do a docker inspect -f {{ .Mounts }} to see all of the mounts on the container.
You can also look at the docker image with a docker inspect, and you'll see the "logs" volume from the Dockerfile VOLUME definition>]

I've built a Docker image for the policy handler without the VOLUME definition in the Dockerfile.  I've deployed it into Vijay's environment, and it comes up properly, without the error relating to a mount.

Apparently there has been some change in the container runtime environment that no longer allows for relative paths in a volume mount.
